answer: The Eiffel Tower opened in Paris on March 31, 1889, as the centerpiece of the 1889 World's Fair. Designed by Gustave Eiffel, it was the tallest man-made structure until 1930. It remains an iconic symbol of France.

results.title: Eiffel Tower opens in Paris | March 31, 1889 - History.com
results.snippet: # Eiffel Tower opens in Paris. On March 31, 1889, the Eiffel Tower is dedicated in Paris in a ceremony presided over by Gustave Eiffel, the tower’s designer, and attended by French Prime Minister Pierre Tirard, a handful of other dignitaries and 200 construction workers. Out of more than 100 designs submitted, the Centennial Committee chose Eiffel’s plan of an open-lattice wrought-iron tower that would reach almost 1,000 feet above Paris and be the world’s tallest man-made structure. Unperturbed, Eiffel completed his great tower under budget in just two years. The elevators were not completed by March 31, 1889, however, so Gustave Eiffel ascended the tower’s stairs with a few hardy companions and raised an enormous French tricolor on the structure’s flagpole. The Eiffel Tower remained the world’s tallest man-made structure until the completion of the Chrysler Building in New York in 1930.

results.title: Eiffel Tower - Wikipedia
results.snippet: # Eiffel Tower. The **Eiffel Tower** (/ˈaɪfəl/ ⓘ *EYE-fəl*; French: *Tour Eiffel*  ⓘ) is a lattice tower on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. During its construction, the Eiffel Tower surpassed the Washington Monument to become by far the tallest human-made structure in the world, a title it held for 41 years until the Chrysler Building in New York City was finished in 1930. According to *The New York Times*, "[at] a time when the [CIC] was helping finance one of the world's best-known landmarks, the Eiffel Tower, as a monument to French liberty, it was choking Haiti's economy, taking much of the young nation's income back to Paris and impairing its ability to start schools, hospitals and the other building blocks of an independent country.".
